content.text: How can I implement an endpoint that securely deletes a user's authentication token when they request a logout?
What happens in the current logic if a valid token is presented but the associated user account has already been hard-deleted?

In your current implementation, if the record exists (`self.model`), it will be deleted even if the related user was removed previously. If you wanted to prevent deleting orphaned tokens, you could add a check here:
That makes sense; we should handle those dangling records more carefully.
